title: Secure Ingress with TLS
description:
## Secure Ingress with TLS

Exposing applications over HTTPS requires a TLS certificate stored as a Kubernetes Secret of type `kubernetes.io/tls`. The Ingress controller uses this secret to terminate TLS connections.

**Your task:**

1. A self-signed TLS certificate has been pre-generated at `/tmp/tls.crt` and `/tmp/tls.key`.
2. A deployment `tls-app` and service `tls-svc` are already running.
3. Create a **TLS Secret** named `app-tls` from the cert files.
4. Create an **Ingress** named `tls-ingress` that:
   - Routes traffic for host `secure.lab.local` to service `tls-svc` on port `80`
   - Terminates TLS using the secret `app-tls`

hints.title: Create the TLS Secret
hints.body: Use `kubectl create secret tls` with `--cert` and `--key` flags pointing to the pre-generated files.

hints.title: Create the TLS Ingress
hints.body: Add a `spec.tls[]` block referencing the secret name alongside `spec.rules[]`.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: tls-ingress
spec:
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - secure.lab.local
    secretName: app-tls
  rules:
  - host: secure.lab.local
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: tls-svc
            port:
              number: 80
EOF
